The three kinds of cubits mentioned above represent in
order the length of 192, 168 and 144 full-grown barley grains.
So 100 medium cubits would be equal to 87| major cubits. The total
height of the 7J storeys of a king's house would be 61c. 21d. Here
Utpala commits a mistake, because he says the total height is 62,6
cubits.
Vi^vakarman lays down that the height of the foundations of
best houses should be 2^- cubits. There is restriction regarding the
height of the storeys of buildings only with two, three and four halls.
In a house of a single hall, the height is equal to its breadth. For,
says Sakra :—
